description_long: This object is a roughly spherical, segmented stone with a distinctive cracked and veined surface, resembling a septarian nodule. The exterior is a pale beige to light brown color, with prominent, irregular tan and cream lines forming polygonal patterns across its surface. One side of the stone is cut or broken open, revealing a striking interior with dark brown to black matrix and lighter, branching mineral veins radiating from the center. The stone's surface is rugged and uneven, with visible indentations and natural weathering, suggesting exposure to the elements. The overall form is compact and solid, with no visible openings or cavities beyond the exposed cross-section. This stone would likely serve as a natural obstacle or decorative feature in an urban park or landscaped area.
description: A segmented, veined stone with a cracked beige exterior and a striking, patterned mineral interior exposed on one side. Its rugged, weathered surface and unique cross-section make it visually distinctive.
description_view_0: Top and bottom views show the cracked, polygonal beige exterior and the exposed interior with dark and light branching mineral veins.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the stone's thickness and the transition from the rough exterior to the exposed mineral interior.
description_view_2: Bottom and top views again, emphasizing the contrast between the solid, veined exterior and the intricate, patterned interior.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, showing the stone's profile and the depth of the exposed mineral section.
attribute_location: urban park landscaping, decorative rock garden, or roadside embankment
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or partially embedded in soil or gravel
attribute_pattern: Irregular polygonal cracking and veining on the exterior, with branching mineral veins and contrasting colors in the exposed interior; the pattern is natural, organic, and visually striking.
attribute_weathering: Rugged and weathered with visible cracks and surface roughness, indicating prolonged exposure to natural elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with manicured urban features but can blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements; may be partially surrounded by soil, gravel, or low vegetation.
